subtopics: Understand the meaning of |x|, sketch the graph of y = |ax + b| and use relations such as |a| = |b| ⟺ a² = b² and |x – a| < b ⟺ a – b < x < a + b when solving equations and inequalities
Divide a polynomial of degree not exceeding 4 by a linear or quadratic polynomial, identifying the quotient and remainder
Use the factor theorem and the remainder theorem to find factors, remainders, solve polynomial equations or evaluate unknown coefficients
Express rational functions in partial fractions with denominators of the form (ax + b)(cx + d)(ex + f), (ax + b)(cx + d)², and (ax + b)(cx² + d)
Use the binomial expansion of (1 + x)ⁿ where n is a rational number and |x| < 1, including adapting the standard series and determining the set of values of x for which the expansion is valid
topics.description: Candidates should understand modulus functions and their application in solving equations and inequalities, perform polynomial division and apply the factor and remainder theorems, decompose rational functions into partial fractions, and use the binomial expansion for rational indices.

subtopics: Understand the relationship between logarithms and indices, and use the laws of logarithms
Understand the definition and properties of eˣ and ln x, including their relationship as inverse functions and their graphs
Use logarithms to solve equations and inequalities in which the unknown appears in indices
Use logarithms to transform a given relationship to linear form, and hence determine unknown constants by considering the gradient and/or intercept
topics.description: Candidates should understand the relationship between logarithms and indices, the properties of eˣ and ln x as inverse functions, use logarithms to solve exponential equations and inequalities, and transform relationships to linear form to determine unknown constants.

subtopics: Understand the relationship of secant, cosecant and cotangent functions to cosine, sine and tangent, and use properties and graphs of all six trigonometric functions for angles of any magnitude
Use the identities sec²θ ≡ 1 + tan²θ and cosec²θ ≡ 1 + cot²θ for simplification, exact evaluation and solving equations
Use the expansions of sin(A ± B), cos(A ± B) and tan(A ± B)
Use the double-angle formulae for sin 2A, cos 2A and tan 2A
Express a cos θ + b sin θ in the forms R sin(θ ± α) and R cos(θ ± α), and apply to solving equations and finding maxima/minima
topics.description: Candidates should understand the reciprocal trigonometric functions (sec, cosec, cot) and their graphs, use trigonometric identities including the Pythagorean identities for sec and cosec, apply compound angle and double angle formulae, and express a cos θ + b sin θ in equivalent R-form.

subtopics: Use the derivatives of eˣ, ln x, sin x, cos x, tan x, tan⁻¹x, together with constant multiples, sums, differences and composites
Differentiate products and quotients using the product rule and quotient rule
Find and use the first derivative of a function defined parametrically
Find and use the first derivative of a function defined implicitly, including in problems involving tangents and normals
topics.description: Candidates should differentiate exponential, logarithmic and trigonometric functions including composites, apply the product and quotient rules, and find derivatives of parametrically and implicitly defined functions including applications to tangents and normals.

subtopics: Integrate e^(ax + b), 1/(ax + b), sin(ax + b), cos(ax + b), sec²(ax + b) and 1/(x² + a²)
Use trigonometrical relationships such as double-angle formulae in carrying out integration
Integrate rational functions by means of decomposition into partial fractions
Recognise an integrand of the form k·f'(x)/f(x) and integrate such functions
Recognise when an integrand can usefully be regarded as a product, and use integration by parts
Use a given substitution to simplify and evaluate either a definite or an indefinite integral
topics.description: Candidates should integrate a range of functions including exponential, trigonometric and rational functions, use trigonometric identities and partial fractions to simplify integrands, recognise and integrate functions of the form f'(x)/f(x), apply integration by parts, and use substitution to evaluate integrals.

subtopics: Locate approximately a root of an equation by means of graphical considerations and/or searching for a sign change
Understand the idea of, and use the notation for, a sequence of approximations which converges to a root of an equation
Understand how a given simple iterative formula of the form xₙ₊₁ = F(xₙ) relates to the equation being solved
Use a given iteration, or an iteration based on a given rearrangement of an equation, to determine a root to a prescribed degree of accuracy
topics.description: Candidates should locate roots of equations graphically and by sign change, understand iterative sequences and their notation, relate iterative formulae to the equations being solved, and apply iteration to determine roots to a required degree of accuracy.

subtopics: Use standard notations for vectors in 2 and 3 dimensions
Carry out addition and subtraction of vectors and multiplication of a vector by a scalar, and interpret these operations in geometrical terms
Calculate the magnitude of a vector, and use unit vectors, displacement vectors and position vectors
Understand the equation of a straight line in the form r = a + tb, and find the equation of a line given sufficient information
Determine whether two lines are parallel, intersect or are skew, and find the point of intersection of two lines when it exists
Use formulae to calculate the scalar product of two vectors, and use scalar products in problems involving lines and points including angles and perpendiculars
topics.description: Candidates should use standard vector notation in two and three dimensions, perform vector operations and interpret them geometrically, find equations of straight lines in vector form, determine relationships between lines, and use the scalar product to solve problems involving angles and perpendiculars.

subtopics: Formulate a simple statement involving a rate of change as a differential equation
Find by integration a general form of solution for a first order differential equation in which the variables are separable
Use an initial condition to find a particular solution
Interpret the solution of a differential equation in the context of a problem being modelled by the equation
topics.description: Candidates should formulate differential equations from statements involving rates of change, solve first-order separable differential equations, use initial conditions to find particular solutions, and interpret solutions in the context of the modelling problem.

subtopics: Understand the idea of a complex number, recall the meaning of real part, imaginary part, modulus, argument, conjugate, and use the condition for equality of two complex numbers
Carry out operations of addition, subtraction, multiplication and division of two complex numbers expressed in Cartesian form x + iy
Use the result that for a polynomial equation with real coefficients, any non-real roots occur in conjugate pairs
Represent complex numbers geometrically by means of an Argand diagram
Carry out operations of multiplication and division of two complex numbers expressed in polar form r(cos θ + i sin θ) = re^(iθ)
Find the two square roots of a complex number
Understand in simple terms the geometrical effects of conjugating a complex number and of adding, subtracting, multiplying and dividing two complex numbers
Illustrate simple equations and inequalities involving complex numbers by means of loci in an Argand diagram
topics.description: Candidates should understand complex numbers including their real and imaginary parts, modulus, argument and conjugate, perform arithmetic operations in Cartesian and polar form, apply the conjugate root theorem for polynomials with real coefficients, represent complex numbers on Argand diagrams, find square roots, understand geometrical effects of operations, and illustrate equations and inequalities as loci.
